I understand your point. However, as per our standard process, we first deploy the item to the staging environment, then submit it to you for review. After receiving your confirmation, we proceed with deploying it to production.

We assumed that the item was tested and validated on staging and that it was working as per your expectations. However, the issue was reported after a few days. column314:
21/Jan/26 10:05 AM;712020:40e61868-0b35-48a8-8a7c-01d6cc90eccc;[~accountid:712020:1eddb609-ce8e-415d-a9f4-4cacf1e9184f] 

Thank you for the clarification and for outlining your standard deployment process.

I understand and acknowledge the importance of validating all changes on the staging environment prior to production deployment, and I agree that this is best practice to maintain system stability.

In this specific case, the combined reports functionality was reviewed on staging and confirmed not to be breaking. However, the issue relates to *exceptional requests for specific clients (GA and APA)*, where validation cannot be fully completed on staging unless the reports are *approved in real time and actually sent out to the client from the production environment*.

Without confirmation from the client that the expected output has been received and meets their requirements, it becomes difficult to conclusively validate such cases while still in staging.

To avoid similar situations going forward, I believe it would be beneficial for us to build a culture where:

* Exceptional workflows are clearly identified early,
* Clarification is proactively sought where staging limitations exist

This will ensure that both teams are aligned and confident that requests are not only deployed but also *fully satisfied and correctly implemented* from a functional and business perspective.

I appreciate your support in making the necessary adjustment for this instance.

Thank you

column316:
22/Jan/26 8:43 AM;557058:a5ff4af3-8ca3-48b2-97b8-555752f6dd3d;Hello [~accountid:712020:40e61868-0b35-48a8-8a7c-01d6cc90eccc] 
We’ve checked current implementation:

* The Combined Report is generated only when the user clicks the “Download Combined Report” button.
* This report is generated on the fly, includes the valuation content only, and is directly downloaded (it is not stored in S3).
* Upon request approval, the system currently generates only the Normal Valuation Report.
* The valuation report is then shared with all associated users.
* No combined report is generated or sent automatically at the approval stage.

*Technical Consideration*
* Generating both the Valuation Report and the Combined Report at the time of approval will increase processing load.
* If done synchronously, this may impact approval performance, as multiple heavy report generations would occur at the same time.
* Additionally, since the combined report needs to be sent automatically, it would require generation and temporary storage (e.g., S3), unlike the current on-click download behavior.

Please let us know if you would like us to proceed with this approach or if further clarification is needed.

column1: Update "Awaiting Documents" Workflow to Show Detailed Reasons & Trigger Specific Notifications. Currently, “Awaiting Documents” is a single checkbox, which limits visibility and does not trigger the correct communications.
column30:
h3. *Requirements*

h4. *1. Replace “Awaiting Documents” checkbox with a dropdown list*

When the user selects *Awaiting Documents*, the following reasons should appear:

*Primary Reasons (show under Awaiting Documents dropdown):*

* No Logbook
* No Insurance Sticker
* No Insurance Instruction Letter

*Secondary Dropdown: “Other”*
Contains:

* No Photos
* Awaiting Invoice

h3. *2. Trigger Notifications Based on Selected Reason*

Selecting *Awaiting Documents + Reason* should automatically send notifications to:

h4. *a. Initiator*

h4. *b. Customer*

h4. *c. Solver*

*BUT with conditions:*

* If the reason is *No Photos*, *DO NOT* notify the customer (since customers are not associated with photo submission).
* For all other reasons, notify all three: initiator, customer, solver.

The notification content must mention the *specific missing document*, e.g.:

* “Pending: No Logbook”
* “Pending: No Insurance Sticker”
* “Pending: Awaiting Invoice”, etc.

h3. *3. Daily Visibility Requirement*

The updated structure must allow valuers to easily see *daily counts and reasons* for pending reports.

h3. *Acceptance Criteria*

* Checkbox replaced with dropdown for *Awaiting Documents*
* “Other” dropdown created with the specified items
* Notifications sent automatically as per the rules
* Customer NOT notified for “No Photos”
* UI clearly shows detailed pending reasons daily
* Tested and confirmed working across all user roles
column300:
24/Nov/25 7:15 AM;557058:a5ff4af3-8ca3-48b2-97b8-555752f6dd3d;Hello [~accountid:712020:40e61868-0b35-48a8-8a7c-01d6cc90eccc] 

We need clarification on the following points regarding this SR:

# *Notification Channels*
When a reason such as *No Logbook*, *No Insurance Sticker*, *Awaiting Invoice*, etc. is selected under _Awaiting Documents_, which channels should the system use to notify the Initiator, Customer, and Solver?

* Email
* SMS
* Push Notification

Please confirm which of these channels should be used.

# *Solver Action on “Awaiting Documents”*
Currently, when a report is marked as *Awaiting Documents*, the solver receives a notification, but there is *no option for the solver to update or resume the valuation*.
Should the solver be able to update the report once the missing documents are received?
Or should this status be updated only by the *Admin/Valuation Manager* instead?

2.*Solver Action on “Awaiting Documents* -

*Trigger Notifications Based on Selected Reason*

We would like to clarify the workflow:

column1: Server upgrade
column30:
As one of the options you gave, we can proceed with the below:

*Option 1: RDS MySQL (Larger Instance)*

* *Instance Type:* Fixed size (manual resize required)
* *Scaling:* Manual upgrade/downgrade
* *Performance:* Standard
* *High Availability:* Multi-AZ optional
* *Cost:*
** t4g.xlarge: ~$200/month (current)
** Proposed upgrade t4g.2xlarge: ~$390/month (base compute + 100GB storage)
* *Best For:* Steady workloads with predictable resource requirements
column300:
05/Nov/25 3:31 PM;557058:666737e2-1a8a-453f-9a14-72a95f4f92cb;[~accountid:712020:40e61868-0b35-48a8-8a7c-01d6cc90eccc] As per our discussion for reducing the storage size of current server here are some challenges which are found.

Challenges Faced in Reducing Storage Size in AWS RDS:

1 : Direct Storage Reduction: In AWS, it is not possible to directly reduce the storage size of an existing RDS instance.

2 : Solution: Create a New RDS Instance: To address this, we need to create a new RDS instance and migrate the data from the current RDS to the new one. There are two approaches to transfer data from the existing RDS server to the new server:

{noformat}2a: Manual Backup and Restore:

-> In this approach, we take a manual backup of the current database and restore it in the new RDS instance. This process requires approximately 3 hours of downtime for the restore operation.

-> The current RDS instance is private, and direct database connections are not allowed except through the Solvit EC2 production server.

-> The backup needs to be stored in the EC2 production server, which requires at least 10 GB of storage for the database dump. Currently, only 5 GB of storage is available on the EC2 production server.

-> To proceed, we either need to:

    -> Increase the storage size of the current EC2 production server.

    -> Or, create a new EC2 server for storing the backup and performing the dump and restore process. If a new server is created, there will be additional charges for that.


2b: Using AWS DMS (Data Migration Service):

-> Alternatively, we can use the AWS Data Migration Service (DMS) to migrate the data. However, this method also requires downtime and introduces risks of data loss during the migration process.{noformat}

3: Post-Migration Changes:

{noformat}-> After creating the new RDS instance, the RDS hostname will change. This requires updating the RDS host details in various places:

    -> In the production environment configuration files.

    -> In the Solvit AWS SDK server configuration files.

    -> The main challenge lies in updating Zoho Analytics after the changes, as there is a risk that the automatic synchronization may fail, since it relies on the old RDS host.{noformat}

So for this, its adviced to upgrade with current storage. At later stage, we will revisit the current infra and also suggest if there are any options where we can reduce the cost.

column1: 1. Initiate Assessment Report
column30:
Anyone with an internet connect can initiate an accident assessment report on the *dashboard*. These users fall into the following categories:

# Customer - isFullService? Via SMS maybe SMS the word “help” to 23624 
# Agent - 
# Broker
# *_Accident Assistance Solver \[pause on this]_*
# *_Assessment Solver  \[pause on this]_*
# Scheduler
# Insurance company



A request can be initiated in the following ways:

# Login in on the dashboard
# SMS short-code eg SMS the word “help” to 23624 

When initiating an accident report, we determine service type based on the channel partners service type. For any request of which the insurance is not one of ours, they have to select the service type and make payment.

{noformat}if(initiated_by.insurance_co is not on our list){
  ask initiator to select service type
  ask customer to pay based on service type
}{noformat}

column1: 2. Schedule request
column30:
Find nearest accident reporting solver.

Solver contacts customer and head to scene of accident

column1: 3. Conduct Accident Assessment
column30: On arrival solver reassures customer of their support, shows empathy, and explain the process. They then start assessment.

column1: 4. Generate Assessment Report
5. Generate billing report
4.1 Field Assessment
Daily in-app notification to solvers showing total pending scheduled jobs
column30:
Solvers are currently missing scheduled jobs because they forget to check their assigned work.

To address this, we need to implement a *daily in-app notification* that is sent to *all solvers* showing the *total number of pending jobs* in their schedule.

The notification should allow the solver to *tap/click through directly to their scheduled requests list* for immediate action.

Example reference: *Request ID 288202* was not actioned due to solver oversight.

h3. *Notification Trigger*

* *Frequency:* Once daily
* *Audience:* All active solvers
* *Delivery method:* In-app notification
* *Schedule:* Daily (exact time to be confirmed — e.g. morning hours when they first open the app after the mandatory updates are filled if there’s any)

h3. *Pending Jobs Definition*

A pending job is any request that:

* Is assigned to the solver
* Is in the solver’s schedule
* Has *not yet been submitted or cancelled*

(Uses same logic as current “Scheduled Requests” list.)

----

h3. *Notification Message (Sample)*

*Title:*

{noformat}Pending Scheduled Jobs
{noformat}

*Body:*

{noformat}You have #COUNT pending job(s) in your schedule.
Tap to view and action them.
{noformat}

Where:

* {{#COUNT}} = total pending scheduled requests for that solver

h3. *Click Action*

When the solver taps the notification:

* Redirect them to:
** *Scheduled Requests screen*
* Filtered to:
** Only their assigned pending jobs

h3. *Expected Behaviour*

* Notification is generated individually per solver.
* Each solver sees their *own pending job count only*.
* If pending jobs = 0:
** No notification is sent.
* Notification should not duplicate multiple times per day.

h3. *Out of Scope*

* Push notification to phone lock screen if already supported.
* No changes to scheduling logic.

h3. *Acceptance Criteria*

* Daily notification sent to all active solvers
* Notification displays correct pending job count per solver
* Clicking notification opens scheduled requests list
* Completed or cancelled jobs are excluded
* Solvers with zero pending jobs do not receive notification

column1: Post Repair Inspection Flow & Report Updates
column30:
We need to introduce a Post Repair Inspection feature to the existing vehicle assessment flow. This will reuse the current Conduct Assessment flow but with modifications.

*Button & Flow Changes:*

Rename “Conduct Assessment” button to “Post Repair Inspection”.

Flow remains the same as the assessment flow, but only the Field Assessment option should be available after clicking the button.

Field Assessment should schedule a solver as currently done.

*Solver Actions:*

Solver to take new (post-repair) photos of the vehicle.

Report must compare Before (from original report) vs After (new photos).

*For each part:*

Keep “Select Part” option.

Replace “Severity” dropdown with “Repair Status” (options: Repaired, Not Repaired, Poorly Repaired).

Add comment section.

Add scrap weight estimate (entered by solver) × cost per KG (fixed at 50 KES/kg).

*Report Changes:*

*_Fields to match reinspection report:_*

Insured Name

Registration Number

Policy Number

Repairer

*_Add generic intro text:_*

“Following instructions received, we re-inspected the vehicle as below:”

*_Each part should display:_*

Status of repair

Before & After photos

Comment section

Scrap weight × cost per KG calculation

*_Download Options:_*

Add new button “Download Post Repair Report”.

Keep the existing “Download Repair Estimate” button for initial assessment workflow.

*_Out of Scope:_*

Changes to existing pre-repair assessment workflow.

Showing before photos in mobile app UI (they will only appear in reports).

*_Expected Outcome:_*

A Post Repair Inspection report that clearly shows before vs after photos, repair status, and additional comments.

Ability for admins to download this as a separate report from the portal.

*_Attachments:_*

Sample report (shared separately on WhatsApp for reference).

column1: We currently generate a normal valuation report, however, we now need to introduce a mechanical valuation report that provides a more in-depth and technical inspection.
column30:
The requirement is to retain our existing report structure and layout, but extend it into a mechanical version by adding more detailed sections.
*Enhancements to Add :*

# *Engine System Checks* – idle, leaks, belts, valve noise, coolant, vibration, water pump, radiator, etc.
# *Transmission System* – gear shifts, clutch slip, torque converter, mountings, leaks.
# *Suspension System* – shocks, bushes, ball joints, springs, noises, anchor points.
# *Braking System* – parking brake, pedal travel, ABS, fluid leaks, effectiveness.
# *Steering System* – tie rods, rack/box condition, self-alignment, knocking, power steering function.
# *Electrical & Lighting* – battery, charging, headlights, brake lights, indicators, dashboard lights.
# *Body Work* – paint, dents, repairs, respray, accident evidence.
# *Cooling & Air Conditioning* – AC operation, radiator fins, hoses.
# *Tyre Condition* – tread depth, per tyre assessment.
# *Defects / Remarks Section* – space for assessor notes on defects found.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* Ability to generate two types of reports:
** *Standard Valuation Report* (current).
** *Mechanical Valuation Report* (extended with technical checks).
* Ensure formatting and branding (Solvit watermark, IRA disclaimer, contact info) remain consistent.
* Mechanical report must clearly show *both valuation and mechanical condition details*.
* Ability to choose “Valuation” or “Mechanical Valuation” during report initiation/inspection.

column1: Create Email Alert for AAR Integration Status Description:  Implement an email alert for AAR integration status updates, matching the format and frequency of Britam integration alerts.
Duplicate requests for two registrations
column30:
We are currently encountering duplicates when a request is initiated with the same registration number but a different phone number, which is why there are numerous pending jobs in the system. 

To address this, we need to ensure that only the registration number serves as the unique identifier and alerts the initiator when trying to initiate an existing pending request in the system.

I have attached a screenshot with an example of such a duplicated request.



cc [~accountid:5f881013c07c880075ad4580]

column1: Initiating a request via API on behalf of another insurance company shows initiator company instead of the company initiated on behalf of, on the dashboard.
column30:
Steps to reproduce:

# Initiate a request and pass an insurance company ID

!Screenshot 2025-05-06 at 13.11.20.png|width=83.33333333333334%,alt="Screenshot 2025-05-06 at 13.11.20.png"!

# The Company shows Test Compnay when it should show the company passed as ID 5

!Screenshot 2025-05-06 at 13.11.11.png|width=83.33333333333334%,alt="Screenshot 2025-05-06 at 13.11.11.png"!

* Have is such that, if initiator is Company A, initiating on behalf of Company B, then Requested by column remains as is, A, but Company column should be B. so for Lami World, Requested by would be Lami World, and Company would be Fidelity
* That way, any actions that are taken on that valuation get communicated to Co. B as per current implementation, eg the valuation appears on B’s dashboard etc
* Also ensure to check that the insurance company is active before initiating a valuation on behalf of it, else send a response saying that this insurance company is inactive.

If we make this change at the point of API initiation, it should automatically then ensure that the insurance company that the job is being initiated on behalf of, gets to see their jobs on the dashboard.

Please call me for clarification

column1: Give solvers scheduling ability
column30:
Current Implementation

* A request is initiated in the system and is listed under “Initiated requests”
* Solver requests a job to be assigned to them as below, by sharing the client details
** !Screenshot 2022-07-19 at 10.53.10.png|width=382,height=677!

* The scheduler finds the request under “Initiated Request”
* The scheduler opens the request and clicks the action button “Schedule this request” 
* Valuation is scheduled as per current implementation, by blasting to any solver or selecting a specific solver

*Proposed implementation*

* Solver has a new menu item “Initiated Request” on their app
* Once clicked, it opens the “Find initiated request” page that has two search inputs:
** Customer number and 
** Vehicle registration. 
** and one button “Search”
* The results design should resemble the current schedules page, but instead of “Start”, the button should read “Schedule”
* On clicking “Schedule”, the initiated request should be scheduled to the solver and appear on their schedule list
* The app should redirect to the schedule page, ordered by the most recently added ScheduedDateTime.
* If the search returns null, a message should be read “Initiated request not found. Would you like to initiate this request?“ With two buttons. “Yes” “No”
* On clicking “No”, the app redirects to the home page
* On clicking “Yes”, the app redirects to the “Initiate Request” page as per [https://solvit-app.atlassian.net/browse/CR-143|https://solvit-app.atlassian.net/browse/CR-143|smart-link] (This will be the next CR)

----

h1. Edit 1.0

* Solver has a new button “Blast” under the “Schedule” button on the sidebar
* On clicking the “Blast” button, a window pops up with two input fields and a search button
*# Vehicle Reg
*## Mandatory 
*## Should only search when more than 6 characters have been entered to eliminate guessing
*## Should ignore spaces
*## Should only return one initiated request whose status is pending
*## If both reg and phone number are entered, return only an exact match
*# Phone number
*## Optional
*## Should only search when more than 10 characters have been entered to eliminate guessing
*## Should ignore spaces
*## Should only return one initiated request whose status is pending
*## If both reg and phone number are entered, return only an exact match
*# Search button
*## On click “Search”
*### if there is a result (it should only return one result as we have validation to ensure that you cannot blast more than one vehicle at once), 
*#### Duplicate the current “Schedule Initiated Request” from the Web App, with the following fields
*##### Solver - Auto-filled as the current solver, and not editable
*##### Customer name - Auto-filled and not editable
*##### Customer number - Auto-filled and not editable
*##### Channel partner - Auto-filled and not editable
*##### Location - input field as per current implementation 
*##### Schedule date - input field as per current implementation 
*##### Schedule time - input field as per current implementation 
*##### Comment - input field as per current implementation 
*##### Schedule button - button field as per current implementation where the solver is known
*##### On clicking the “Schedule” button, the initiated request should be scheduled to the solver and appear on their schedule list as per the current implementation
*#### onSuccess - The app should redirect to this new schedule’s specific page as per the current implementation so that the solver can choose to “Start” the valuation without having to search for it again
*#### onFail - The app should give the solver the option to try again by throwing an error message “An error has occurred. Try again”, and on clicking:
*##### Retry - The app refreshes and the API request is made again.
*##### Cancel - The app refreshes and goes to the home page

[~accountid:557058:666737e2-1a8a-453f-9a14-72a95f4f92cb] kindly give hours based on this edit and remember our budget is about 50 - 60 hours max. Thanks.



----

h1. Edit 2.0

# Solver has a new button “Blast” under the “Schedule” button on the sidebar
# On clicking the “Blast” button, a window pops up with {color:#0747a6}three{color} input fields and a search button
## Vehicle Reg
### {color:#0747a6}Mandatory  {color}
### {color:#0747a6}Minimum 6 characters{color}
### {color:#0747a6}Ignore space{color}
## Phone number
### {color:#0747a6}Optional{color}
### {color:#0747a6}Numbers only{color}
### {color:#0747a6}Minimum 10 characters{color}
### {color:#0747a6}Ignore space{color}
## {color:#0747a6}Insurance Company dropdown{color}
### {color:#0747a6}Mandatory{color}
### {color:#0747a6}This will ensure that there is a maximum of one result returned from the query, thanks to {color}[https://solvit-app.atlassian.net/browse/CR-46|https://solvit-app.atlassian.net/browse/CR-46|smart-link]{color:#0747a6} {color}
## Search button
### On click “Search”
#### Should only search when more than 6 characters have been entered in Reg to eliminate guessing. If characters are less than six, add the error message “Vehicle registration should be a minimum of 6 characters”
#### Should only search when more than 10 characters have been entered in the phone number to eliminate guessing. If characters are less than 10, add the error message “Phone number should be a minimum of 10 characters”
#### Should ignore spaces
#### Should only return max one initiated request whose status is pending
#### If there is a result (it should only return one result as we have validation to ensure that you cannot blast more than one vehicle at once for a particular channel partner), 
##### Duplicate the current “Schedule Initiated Request” from the Web App, with the following fields
###### Solver - Auto-filled as the current solver, and not editable
###### Customer name - Auto-filled and not editable
###### Customer number - Auto-filled and not editable
###### Channel partner - Auto-filled and not editable
###### Location - input field as per current implementation 
###### Schedule date - input field as per current implementation 
###### Schedule time - input field as per current implementation 
###### Comment - input field as per current implementation 
###### Schedule button - button field as per current implementation where the solver is known
###### On clicking the “Schedule” button, the initiated request should be scheduled to the solver and appear on their schedule list as per the current implementation
#### {color:#0747a6}If there is no result, show an alert with the message, “No initiated valuation found.” with two buttons:{color}
##### {color:#0747a6}If they click “Initiate”, go to this flow  {color}[https://solvit-app.atlassian.net/browse/CR-143|https://solvit-app.atlassian.net/browse/CR-143|smart-link]{color:#0747a6} {color}
##### {color:#0747a6}If they click “Cancel”, redirect to the “Schedule” page{color}
#### onSuccess of {color:#0747a6}either schedule or initiate{color} - The app should redirect to this new schedule’s specific page as per the current implementation so that the solver can choose to “Start” the valuation without having to search for it again
#### onFail of {color:#0747a6}either schedule or initiate{color}- The app should give the solver the option to try again by throwing an error message “An error has occurred. Try again”, and on clicking:
##### Retry - The app refreshes and the API request is made again.
##### Cancel - The app refreshes and goes to the home page

[~accountid:557058:666737e2-1a8a-453f-9a14-72a95f4f92cb] kindly provide hours based on this edit for both CRs.

----

h1. Edit 3.0

[~accountid:5f881013c07c880075ad4580] kindly review the following changes

After deploying this CR[https://solvit-app.atlassian.net/browse/CR-143|https://solvit-app.atlassian.net/browse/CR-143|smart-link] , solver’s now get a message that the job they are trying to initiate is already in the system. We’d like to make it such that, they can pull the job to their schedule and/or edit the schedule details to the point where they can reschedule to another solver. Kindly make the following changes.

* If the job is already initiated and status == Pending, 
** Edit the alert message to say, “This job is already initiated, would you like to put it on your schedule?”
** Show two buttons
*** If they click “Ok” Put the job on the solver’s schedule 
*** If they click “Cancel”, go back to valuation page

* Give solver the ability to reassign a job that’s on their tab to another solver 
** When they open the specific valuation’s page, add a button “reassign” next to the start button
** On click “Reassign” open a drop-down form below on the same page with the inputs
**# Mandatory drop-down input labelled “Edit schedule”, with three options, “Self“ - default, “Other solver” and “Other location”
**## On select or if default remails as “Self“, whatever changes they make, eg date and/or comment just updates the schedule details
**## On Select “Solver”,  - Show an input field where they can type the solver name, background search and show a list of matches that they can then select from
**## On select “Location” - Show an input field where they can type the location name, background search and show a list of matches that they can then select from
**### If no solver is found in location, add the reason “No Solver Found“ that should appear in this drop-down, and send an email to the initiator [will send email body]

!Screenshot 2025-01-29 at 13.41.36.png|width=83.28690807799443%,alt="Screenshot 2025-01-29 at 13.41.36.png"!
**# Mandatory pre populate “Date/Time” input field to edit the schedule date/time
**# Prepopulated “Comments” text field to edit the comments
**# Red button labeled “Remove from schedule”, which if clicked, gives the dowpdown to choose reason to remove from schedule on save, removes the job from solvers’s schedule. If status was “Solver accept“, it should go back to “Pending”
**# Save button that on Click, give a summary of what the solver has selected, with confirm buttons:
**## Save
**## Cancel

column1: Integrate Tende Pay
column30:
What: Integrate Tende Pay into our payment flow

When: Customer clicks payment link SMS or when solver is about to complete valuation

How: 

# When the customer clicks “Payment via sms” as below,  they are re-routed to Tende Payment Gateway to complete the payment process.

!WhatsApp Image 2022-04-25 at 2.43.22 PM.jpeg|width=716,height=1600!

2. When solver is above to complete valuation, we change the following page as follows:

# Hide Mpesa confirmation code input box,.. this shall be autofilled from Tende Callback URL
# Add new input field for customer mobile number:
## Should be editable, in incase the customer under who’s number was initiated, is not the same as the one paying
## Should be validated to ensure the solver does not make mistake ie grey-out below button until the number is valid
# Add an uneditable field on the amount to pay
# Add a button “Request payment” that sends a payment request SMS to the number solver has entered in a. above
# Disable submit button and only show it after the  “Confirm payment” checkbox == true. 
## Can be checked by solver if they visually see the client’s Mpesa message
## if(confirm_payment \!= true), then can be checked by Tende callback URL on receipt of payment
# “Confirm payment” checkbox == true, enable Submit button else disable.

!WhatsApp Image 2022-04-25 at 2.44.44 PM.jpeg|width=716,height=1600!

Share design of this for approval before development. 

Will share Tende Documentation upon receipt.

[~accountid:5ae9ae257b296356232e1282] kindly share timelines for this.

column1: Give Customer the ability to initiate a valuation request
column30:
Currently, insurance companies share a valuation letter with instructions to the customer on where to take their vehicle for valuation. We want to explicitly inform them how to initiate a valuation request upon receiving the valuation letter by sending the channel partner ID to 23624.

The AfricasTalking API then forwards the response to our API, where we grab the Client Cell, and Channel partner ID and send a custom link with cell number and channel partner ID in URL so that this information is passed onto the web page.

See the UX here [https://solvit-app.atlassian.net/browse/CR-16|https://solvit-app.atlassian.net/browse/CR-16|smart-link] 



 See link to updated flowchart ->  [*Flowchart*|https://lucid.app/lucidchart/6820e484-a1ed-41a3-9eb0-6e13ce182fc9/edit?viewport_loc=117%2C-1449%2C5774%2C2565%2C-Hl-AYTOHW-3&invitationId=inv_995d2884-b1db-4719-8629-976e80b5bb44]

Sample AfricasTalking API response message: 

*Sample response*

\{

  date: 'Wed Jul 14 14:24:53 EAT 2021',

  from: '+25470000000',

  id: 'ATIx_AAAAAAAAAAA',

  networkCode: '62120',

  linkId: 'YuLSusjkZ8O17U4UZ7RY9mq+7pjOA29oWH2WjxGrGuU',

  text: 'test',

  to: '+NUMBER'

}

column1: Give solvers the ability to reassign a scheduled valuation
column30:
Currently, a solver can only start a request. But sometimes after contacting a client, they find that the vehicle is out of reach. In that case, we would like to give the solver the ability to *Reschedule* the request to another specific solver, or by blasting. See revised flow:

[https://lucid.app/lucidchart/6820e484-a1ed-41a3-9eb0-6e13ce182fc9/edit?viewport_loc=1207%2C4%2C2368%2C1052%2CXy.MM3nrmicm&invitationId=inv_995d2884-b1db-4719-8629-976e80b5bb44|https://lucid.app/lucidchart/6820e484-a1ed-41a3-9eb0-6e13ce182fc9/edit?viewport_loc=1207%2C4%2C2368%2C1052%2CXy.MM3nrmicm&invitationId=inv_995d2884-b1db-4719-8629-976e80b5bb44|smart-link]

column1: Send customer SMS when solver force-updates request status to unreachable / not picking / not ready
column30:
Currently, the system sends an SMS notification to the customer only when an admin updates a request status to Not Ready.

This behavior needs to be replicated for solvers.

When a solver performs a force update from the mobile app, the system should automatically send an SMS to the customer if the solver selects any of the following statuses:

# Unreachable
# Not Picking
# Not Ready

This ensures customers are informed in real time when the solver is unable to reach them.

*Trigger Condition*

SMS should be sent when all conditions below are met:

* Update is done by solver
* Update is done via force update on the solver app
* Status updated to one of:
** Unreachable
** Not Picking
** Not Ready

*SMS Content:*

Hi #CUSTOMER, we tried reaching you. 

It only takes 15 minutes with our nearest solver. 

Call 0769737442 / 0115592037 now.

*Expected Behaviour:*

SMS should be sent immediately after solver submits the force update.

Customer phone number should be picked from the request record.

Message should not be duplicated if the same status is saved multiple times consecutively.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

# When solver force-updates status to Unreachable, customer receives SMS
# When solver force-updates status to Not Picking, customer receives SMS
# When solver force-updates status to Not Ready, customer receives SMS
# Admin workflow remains unchanged

column1: Solvit Handover Notes - Esther Wango
column30:
*Prepared by:* Esther Wango
*Role:* Technical Lead / Product Manager

h2. Role of Technical Lead / Product Manager at Solvit

This role combines *technical leadership* and *product management*, bridging business needs with system implementation.

*Technical Leadership*

* Translate business processes into system design and technical specifications (valuation, assessment, reporting, APIs).
* Guide technology choices and oversee architecture.
* Ensure system stability and resolve bugs logged in Jira.
* Monitor integrations and technical dependencies with external partners.

*Product Management*

* Map processes and workflows to define end-to-end valuation and assessment steps.
* Gather requirements from stakeholders and document them as Jira stories, tasks, and epics.
* Prioritize backlog items balancing bug fixes, features, and enhancements.
* Align business stakeholders and development teams to ensure delivery of business value.
* Implement dashboards in Zoho Analytics to provide management visibility and operational insights.

*Hybrid Responsibility*

* Beyond a traditional Product Manager, this role involved hands-on technical contribution (system design, integrations, analytics implementation).
* Beyond a traditional Technical Lead, this role included business alignment (process mapping, backlog prioritization, requirement validation).
* In practice, the role has been about ensuring the *legacy system keeps running, the new valuation system is designed correctly, and management has visibility through analytics.*

h2. Role Overview

Over the last 5 years at Solvit, my work has focused on:

* Mapping and documenting valuation processes to inform system design.
* Driving the build of the valuation system, ensuring alignment with business workflows.
* Leading requirements gathering for functional modules 
* Raising and tracking bugs, stories, and tasks in Jira, ensuring issues were resolved or escalated.
* Implementing Zoho Analytics dashboards for visualization of valuation data.
* Coordinating Zoho Creator side developments for lightweight workflow extensions.

h2. Key Metrics

* *Jira items reported:* 1,094
* *Closure rate:* ~91.5% (Resolved/Deployed/Verified)
* *Median cycle time:* 0 days, 75th percentile: 12 days
* *Issue types:* Tasks (~570), Stories (~180), Bugs (~200), Epics (~18), Subtasks (~110)

*Throughput by year:*

* 2020–2021: Process mapping and early valuation fixes.
* 2022–2023: Major push on valuation workflows, design of new valuation system, Assessment Module build, API/integration tasks.
* 2024–2025: Zoho Analytics dashboards, refinement of apps (Ref Agent, Solver), backlog clean-up.

h2. Major Project Areas & Work Delivered

*System Design (New Valuation System)*

* Helped translate mapped processes into system architecture and requirements.
* Defined modules (Valuation, Assessment, Reporting, APIs).
* Contributed to functional specifications ensuring technical design matched business rules.

*Accident & Assessment Module (Completed)*

* Generated assessment reports, improved accident/assessment workflows, and deployed modules.
* Unified handling of accidents and assessments within valuation workflows.
* Remaining backlog: automation of scheduled reports, minor UX adjustments, bug fixes.

*Valuation Processes*

* Stabilized legacy workflows and aligned them with future system design.
* Tickets addressed flow amendments, and request features.

*API & Integrations*

* Designed and logged orchestration features.
* Oversaw webhooks and partner integrations.

*Analytics/Dashboards*

* Implemented Zoho Analytics dashboards for volumes, distribution, and success rates.
* Future: expand with drill-downs and automation.

*Ref Agent App / Solver App*

* Documented flows and refined user-facing behavior.

h2. Outstanding Issues & Risks

* *Legacy PHP System:* Fragile and prone to downtime; ongoing support required until migration is complete.
* *Data Completeness:* Some valuation request fields not consistently captured, affecting reporting. Will be fixed in new build
* *Backlog in Jira:* ~150 items remain unresolved or in backlog

h2. Recommendations

* Triage the backlog to clear duplicates and compare if factored in new build.
* Continue refining process documentation as the system evolves.
* Maintain support for the legacy valuation system until migration is fully done.
* Keep Jira backlog prioritized and visible.
* Maintain and expand Zoho Analytics dashboards for decision-making.
* Leverage Zoho Creator for quick workflow fixes.
* Advance the new valuation system design into implementation.
* Keep business rules documented to avoid knowledge gaps.

column1: Enhancing Solvit API to accomodate brokers
column30:

Let's remove this and send status emails to initiator. That way, because we have added Brokers as Insurance Companies, they too get all communication.

## Pending - 1 (When a job is successfully initiated)
## Ongoing - 2 (When a job is scheduled to a solver)
## Complete - 3  (When a job is approved)
# Update Swagger Docs such that they reflect the updated variables

column1: RFP Addedndum
column30:
The Solvit Platform is a multi-tenant SaaS product. Vendors must propose an architecture that:

* Supports securely isolating tenant data and configurations
* Enforces tenant scoping in all APIs and data queries
* Enables onboarding new tenants (companies) via configuration rather than code deployments
* Supports at least two initial production tenants (Kenya, Uganda) with ability to scale
* Allows future evolution from shared database to separate schema or database per tenant
* Supports tenant-level settings (e.g., services offered, limits, branding)

column1: Have the Valuations tab display ALL Normal and Fleet vehicles, so it's easy for a user to search for either using the registration.
column30:
*The problem*
It’s impossible to search for a vehicle under fleet if you 

1) You don't know it was done under a fleet and 

2) You don't know the fleet name. 

*Proposed solution*

Have the Valuations tab display both Normal and Fleet vehicles, so it's easy for a user to search for either using the registration. This on the Web App and for all authorised users eg Solvit Staff, Insurance Companies, Brokers and Agents etc

column30:

_Note: These endpoints already exist within the system; the development team’s task is solely to expose them securely._

||#||Endpoint||Method||Purpose||

column1: 36hrs - Give solver the ability to correct a rejected job
column30:
h2. Current implementation

* When a valuer rejects a job, it’s added onto the solvers reject list on solver app
* There is no action that the solver can take

h2. Change request 

We’d like the solver to have the ability to fix the job, by having it load all the information that had been submitted, then they can fix the error, eg retake a certain photo, and submit as usual:

# Web app - when valuer rejects a job, add a mandatory input dropdown with the following pre-populated list:
## Photos not clear
## Captured VIN plate instead of Chassis number
## Captured VIN plate instead of Engine number
## Capture Trip instead of Odometer reading
## Other 
### Option for valuer to type in - we shall then use this input to populate the above list
# Solver App
## For each job on rejected list, add click functionality that opens a new Rejected job’s details page.
## Duplicate all the information from _Scheduled job details page_ 
## Add request ID as the first item on _Scheduled job details page_
## Add request ID as the first item on _Rejected job details page_
## Add “Reject reason” as entered by the valuer on _Rejected job details page_ on the last line
## Add “Rectify button”, that once clicked:
### Changes the status of the job from “Rejected” to “Paused”
### Moves the job from “Rejected” list to “Scheduled” list
### Should now have a “Continue”, button that works as per current implementation
### Keeps a count of how many times a job has been rejected and paused

column1: 28hrs - Give CS Team the ability to remove a job from solvers' schedule and put it back on initiate request list
column30:
h2. Current implementation

* When a customer clicks a link and the job gets blasted to the solver. 
* The job then goes back to initiate the request list as shown below, and the CS team can reassign

!Screenshot 2024-01-30 at 14.37.49.png|width=1440,height=784!

h2. Change request

We’d like to give this ability to Solvers for even jobs that are on their schedule on Solver App

* So if a job’s status is “Scheduled” and is appearing on the solver’s schedule, add a button “Cancel” next to the “Start” button
* OnClick “Cancel” button, open a pop-up window with mandatory fields 
** Drop down for “Reason” as per current implementation
**  Button “Remove from schedule”*
*** On clicking the “Remove from schedule” button, 
**** The job’s status changes to “Pending”,
**** “Assign Request Status” changes to “Cancelled by Solver”
**** Job appears on Initiate list for CS team
**** The reason should show as per the solver input
**** On success, the app redirects to scheduled page
**** On error, alert error message and redirect to the schedule page

column1: Implement new functionality where a repeat job is automatically scheduled to the previous solver, if their app is on. If the app is not on, it's scheduled per current implementation.
column300: 29/Jan/25 1:15 PM;5f76d891e31b69006f9b1873;[~accountid:557058:666737e2-1a8a-453f-9a14-72a95f4f92cb] provide hours for this
column301:
29/Jan/25 1:37 PM;557058:666737e2-1a8a-453f-9a14-72a95f4f92cb;[~accountid:5f76d891e31b69006f9b1873] Can you clarify the app is on mean?

There is following case where the app is on.

# The solver is logged in and his status is active and the app is in forground (mean his application is open)

If is app is on, do we need to provide the same pop up, where solver can start, schedule or cancel the request?

----

In the following following cases the app is considered off.

# The solver is logged in and his status is active and the app is in background (mean the application is in backgroud)
# The solver is logged in and his status is active and the app is in killed (mean the application is in killed state)

Also note, if solver will be working on another request then the request will be scheduled.
column302:
31/Jan/25 10:46 AM;5f76d891e31b69006f9b1873;[~accountid:557058:666737e2-1a8a-453f-9a14-72a95f4f92cb] 

There is following case where the app is on.

# The solver is logged in and his status is active and the app is in forground (mean his application is open) - *this is considered on* 

If is app is on, do we need to provide the same pop up, where solver can start, schedule or cancel the request? - *yes*

----

In the following following cases the app is considered off.

# The solver is logged in and his status is active and the app is in background (mean the application is in backgroud) - *Solver should still receive an sms informing then that they’ve been assigned a repeat job* 
# The solver is logged in and his status is active and the app is in killed (mean the application is in killed state) - *Solver should still receive an sms informing then that they’ve been assigned a repeat job* 

Also note, if solver will be working on another request then the request will be scheduled. - *yes, added to this solvers schedule*

column1: Add Sentry logging to our app
column30:
We are adopting a more hands on approach to maintaining our Solvit Application so as to know where to put in efforts in terms of maintenance. 

Monolog and Log Viewer are integrated into our Laravel application and are maintained in-house. Sentry, as an external service, offloads much of the maintenance and scalability challenges, though it introduces an external dependency.

To that effect, kindly install Sentry logging that will give us visibility of logs without too much effort. Follow the documentation from the link below.

[https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/php/guides/laravel/other-versions/laravel5/|https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/php/guides/laravel/other-versions/laravel5/|smart-link] 

dsn=[https://6b40652646544ca8b0f3f64557c562f6@o4508970659807232.ingest.us.sentry.io/4508970662559744|https://6b40652646544ca8b0f3f64557c562f6@o4508970659807232.ingest.us.sentry.io/4508970662559744]

When Sentry is installed in the application, it will also be active when you are developing or running tests.

We don’t want errors to be sent to Sentry when we are developing or running tests. To avoid this, set the DSN value to {{null}} to disable sending errors to Sentry.

You can also do this by not defining {{SENTRY_LARAVEL_DSN}} in your {{.env}} or by defining it as {{SENTRY_LARAVEL_DSN=null}}.

Let me know the efforts it will take to configure this

column1: Solvit app image optimisation strategy for performance improvement
column30:
# First, I ran a speed test to determine my internet speed. It looks ok.

!Screenshot 2023-10-31 at 11.19.41.png|width=793,height=354!

# Second, I opened a valuation using Chrome dev tools to observe the network calls, and then I ran a comparison against another image-heavy site, in this case, [https://www.shutterstock.com/|https://www.shutterstock.com/|smart-link]. *NOTE:* This is not a fair comparison as Shutterstock is a more mature application, but the goal is to work towards such optimisations on our end too.

*Solvit - loading one valuation*

!Screenshot 2023-10-31 at 11.23.56.png|width=100%!

*Shutterstock - loading a search* [https://www.shutterstock.com/search/tesla|https://www.shutterstock.com/search/tesla|smart-link] 

!Screenshot 2023-10-31 at 12.27.37.png|width=100%!

*Comparison* 

|| ||*Shutterstock*||*Solvit*||
|Load time|19.86ms|6.1 minutes|
|Resources|5.9mb|38mb|
|Image type|WEBP|JPG|
|Requests|223|78|
|DOM content loadtime|1.60ms|9.60s|
|No of images load|~115|23|
|Largest image|46.1kb @ 192MS|2.6mb @ 2.7m|
|Response headers|Content-Type: image/webp
Server: AmazonS3  ✅ 
Via: 1.1 [374f9b0c87bf947b0c84e865b647ffae.cloudfront.net|http://374f9b0c87bf947b0c84e865b647ffae.cloudfront.net] (CloudFront)
X-Cache: {color:#ff5630}Hit from cloudfront{color}|Content-Type: image/jpg
Server: AmazonS3  ✅|

h2. *Proposed image strategy to improve loading speed for Solvit*

* Save original-sized images, but don’t serve them
* Compress and serve images into two sizes (thumbnails ~ 50kbs, full-size ~600kb)
** Thumbnail  sample → [https://www.shutterstock.com/image-photo/seaside-ca-may-29-2016-260nw-593728589.jpg|https://www.shutterstock.com/image-photo/seaside-ca-may-29-2016-260nw-593728589.jpg] 
** Fullsize sample -> [https://www.shutterstock.com/shutterstock/photos/593728589/display_1500/stock-photo-seaside-ca-may-white-tesla-model-x-with-falcon-wing-doors-open-charging-at-593728589.jpg|https://www.shutterstock.com/shutterstock/photos/593728589/display_1500/stock-photo-seaside-ca-may-white-tesla-model-x-with-falcon-wing-doors-open-charging-at-593728589.jpg]
* Serve compressed images/media/docs via a CDN → [https://aws.amazon.com/cloudfront/|https://aws.amazon.com/cloudfront/|smart-link] - *1 TB of data transfer out* with the [+AWS Free Tier+|https://aws.amazon.com/cloudfront/pricing/?loc=ft#AWS_Free_Usage_Tier]
* Implement a lazy loading strategy → [https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Performance/Lazy_loading|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Performance/Lazy_loading|smart-link] 

[~accountid:5f881013c07c880075ad4580] let me know when you have a moment to go over the above-proposed changes before briefing the dev team.

column1: Valuation Logic Code
Add the ability for admin to allocate channel partners to the Solvit customer team, and add this as a filter on the initiate requests page
column30:
We’d like to edit the [Initiate Request|https://login.solvit.limited/admin/initiate-request] page as follows:

# Add a new filer *“Allocated to”* (new table)
## Create a table called “Allocations” under User Administration, where we can allocate channel partners to the Solvit customer team. Admin should be able to add/edit the table.
## This table will be used as a filter in the Initiated Requests List to enable a customer service team member to only view their allocated channel partner’s requests
## The filter should have the “Select option”, and then the names of the team members in a drop-down. 
## Selecting the team member's name should return only the channel partners that that team member is allocated. 
## This is the SQL query used to generate the table we use in Zoho analytics to generate this table. the table should have 3 columns.  
### Insurance Company ID, referencing insurance_company_master table
### Insurance Company Name, referencing insurance_company_master table
### Allocated to, referencing the Staff list table 
[https://analytics.zoho.com/open-view/2054533000008731944|https://analytics.zoho.com/open-view/2054533000008731944|smart-link]
### {code:sql}SELECT
	irm."id" as ID,
	irm."name" as Channel_partner,
	if(irm."id"  IN ( 3  , 5  , 22  , 60  ), 'Irene', if(irm."id"  IN ( 50  ), 'Juliet', if(irm."id"  IN ( 7  , 17  , 53  , 10  , 18  , 57  ), 'Joyce', if(irm."id"  IN ( 23  , 26  , 48  , 28  , 14  , 61  , 64  ), 'Dorcas', if(irm."id"  IN ( 49  , 52  , 55  , 54  ), 'Ami', if(irm."id"  IN ( 11  , 44  , 45  , 24  , 8  ), 'Flaviah', 'Other')))))) as Allocated_to
FROM  "insurance_company_master" AS  irm {code}
# Add new column “Scheduled date” 
## We want to highlight jobs that have been scheduled and not started. Use the following criteria:
## {noformat}if(scheduled_datetime < now_datetime && status == scheduled){
  The scheduled dates cells and status should be coloured red as shown below
}{noformat}

||*#Request ID?*||*Requested Date!*||*Schedule Date?*||*…*||*Status!*||
|123|{{2022-12-16}}|{color:#bf2600}*16/12/2022*{color}
{color:#bf2600}*4:30PM*{color}|…|{color:#bf2600}Scheduled{color}|

column1: Automate Valuation Notifications and Simplify Agent Handling
column30:
*Problem statements*

# The Customer Service (CS) team currently sends the valuation report to the reference agent manually upon approval. This needs to be done specifically by the CS team - where they log in as reference agent to initiate requests.

# Reference agents and customers do not receive an email notification when a valuation is initiated or approved

# Reference agents are many, and we don’t have all of them in our database.

 

*Proposed Solution*

# Change the "Select Agent" field from the drop-down on the valuation request page of Customer service (The user logged in) to the "Reference Agent Email” text field, to enable the initiator to add the reference agent's email address, mandatory*

# Send an email notification to reference agents and customers when a valuation is initiated or approved

# Upon initiation of a request, if the ref agent does not exist, send an email to the ref agent inviting them, to create an account by resetting their password.

column1: Do not bill: (Post production). On solvers app, when you take an image, then retake it, the 2nd image does not appear as the current, only the initially taken image.
column30:
Do not bill: (Post-deployment). In the Solvers app, when capturing an image and retaking it, the second image does not display as the current one; instead, the originally taken image remains.

When a solver begins a task and captures an image, they check if it's acceptable (i.e., not blurry or unclear). If the image is unsatisfactory, they retake it. However, the second image does not replace the first one, causing solvers to submit incorrect images.

Please address this issue as it is causing problems.

column1: Adding unique identifier after vehicle registration for all ALL outgoing systems sms's
column30:
We need to make a Change request for adding a unique identifier after vehicle registration for all outgoing systems SMSs eg a message like 

Hi ${firstname} ${lastname}, ${channel_partner} has created your Solvit account to value ${vehicle_reg} at your location. Click ${link} to proceed. Have your ID, PIN & Logbook

should change to read

Hi ${firstname} ${lastname}, ${channel_partner} has created your Solvit account to value ${vehicle_reg} , ref {request_id}-${timestamp} on ${cellphone}* at your location. Click ${link} to proceed. Have your ID, PIN & Logbook

eg Hi Charles Njeru, Fidelity has created your Solvit account to value KDM 783V, ref 167345-0102241019 on 0721***728 at your location. Click ${link} to proceed. Have your ID, PIN & Logbook

The ref ID combines the request ID and timestamp in the form of ddmmyyhhmm, eg req ID 167345 and 01/02/24/10:19 becomes 167345-0102241019. Time should be in 24hr format.

So ALL outgoing SMSes should have this added, ref 167345-0102241019 on 0721*728 added after the vehicle reg.

column1: Round Robin for Customer Care team on the initiated requests page.
column30:
I would like to propose an enhancement to our system that involves the addition of a new column, 'Customer Service', to the initiated requests page ( [SolvIT | Initiated Request List|https://login.solvit.limited/admin/initiate-request] ). This column will be positioned between the existing 'Vehicle' and 'Type' columns. Its purpose is to facilitate the tracking of customer care members responsible for overseeing initiated requests and ensuring their proper follow-through.

*To elaborate on the workflow, I recommend implementing the following assignment process:*

# *Automated Assignment for Insurance Company Requests*: Any request initiated by insurance companies should be automatically assigned to a customer service member. Each customer service member should be allocated three requests per person, following a round-robin distribution method. This approach ensures equitable distribution of requests among our team members.For every request initiated by insurance companies, they should be automatically assigned to a customer service member each getting 3 requests per person in the form of round-robin. 
# *Self initiated request by the customer care member*: In cases where a request is initiated by a member of our customer care team, the same customer care member should be assigned the request they have initiated. This approach ensures continuity in handling such requests. 
# *Filter drop-down*; Add a filter drop-down labeled 'Customer Service'. This drop-down filter will enable users to sort and view customer care members based on their associated initiated requests, streamlining the monitoring and management process.

column1: Improve the Solver rating feature by adding a few more data collection points like client, valuer and customer service team, and consolidating this on the app
column30:
h1. See CRs to be worked on and completed in March.

h2. *1. Solver rating* 

h3. We’d like to improve the Solver rating feature by adding a few more data collection points.

# *Client Feedback:* Once the solver completes a valuation*,* clients are to be sent an SMS with a single question to rank the solver’s performance in various areas. E.g. SMS with a link that takes you to a page similar to the same review page that's currently on the Solvit customer app
## This will weigh 40% of the total rating, so the total score from the customer will be (sum of ratings / no. of questions) * 40%
# *Customer Service feedback* - same as valuers feedback below, but if logged in user is customer service, then this is an optional form. 
# *Valuer's Feedback:* 
## At the bottom of the valuation page, add a section for the valuer to rate the solver. We shall have 5 questions with a scale of 1 (Very bad) and 5 (Very good), (Use dummy questions for now), to be rated on a scale of 1 to 5. 
## This will have a weight of 60%. So the total score from the valuer and customer service, will be (sum of ratings / no. of questions) * 60%
# *Feedback Consolidation on Solver App:* 
## There should be a dedicated solver rating for each valuation, which will be a total of 1a and 3b for now. Will add 2. after it is implemeted
## This score is then loaded on the solver app’s homepage to see their rating

----

h2. *-2. Handing pending jobs-*

{panel:bgColor=#e3fcef}
Moved to separate CR
{panel}

# -Show the monetary value of pending jobs (All jobs on the solver’s list whose status is not complete).-
# -Limit the number of paused jobs a Solver can have to 10. Once this limit is reached, they can no longer receive any new jobs and are advised to complete the jobs on their scheduled list.-

*-Todos-*

# -Add a row on the homepage of the solver Solver App that shows:-
## -The monetary value of pending jobs, which is equal to the total number of scheduled jobs pending * solver fee-
## -If “Paused jobs” are equal to or greater than 10-
### -Add a column with red text that reads “Paused jobs are equal to or greater than 10. Complete these jobs to get assigned new ones.”-
### -Solver cannot be assigned work until paused jobs are less than 10-

|{color:#bf2600}-Paused jobs are equal to or greater than 10. Complete these jobs to get assigned new ones.-{color}|-Pending jobs-
{color:#bf2600}*-23-*{color}|-Earning potentual-
{color:#bf2600}*-9,200-*{color}|

----

h2. -3. Solver schedule page on the App-

{panel:bgColor=#eae6ff}
Covered in [https://solvit-app.atlassian.net/browse/CR-284|https://solvit-app.atlassian.net/browse/CR-284|smart-link]


{panel}

-Edit the solver schedule page such that:-

-a) The schedule page becomes a list, and each row has the request ID, vehicle reg and status.-

||*-Request ID-*||*-Registration-*||*-Status-*||
|-123223-|-KBZ123Z-|-Pending-|
|-100234-|-KBG322R-|-Paused-|
|-….-|-….-|-….-|

# -The table should be paginated and load only 10 pages at a time which the pagination navigating icons-
# -Add a search filter. The user should be able to find a valuation by:-
#* -Request ID-
#* -Registration-
#* -Customer number-
# -Add sort functionality-
#* -The user should be able to sort ascending or descending by:-
#** -Request ID-
#** -Status-

-b) Add a valuations details page-

# -When a user clicks a valuation row, they are navigated to a new page.-
# -The title of the page should read “Valuation details” The page should list the valuation details as they appear on the current schedule. Use the same design-
# -On the above, add a list of the available KYCs so that the solver knows which KYCs are available, and which they need to ask for. They should not be able to open the KYCS here.-
# -On request notification, indicate the required KYC document that has not been provided eg”,.. request for Logbook”. If logbook is missing from KYCs. If all, list them separated by commas.-
# -Customer service should also be able to see the same on the initiated request list and have the ability to view each document. On the Web App, on Initiate Page, below “Payment Type”, add another line item labelled KYCs, which lists the available documents, separated by commas, with an eye icon for each, such that when clicked, the user can see the document in a floating window.-

!Screenshot 2023-03-13 at 10.37.23.png|width=412,height=291!

----

h2. -4. Customer SMS for repeat jobs-

-If the valuation is a repeat job, then the SMS that goes to the customer shouldn’t have the request for documentation i.e. ID, DL and Logbook-

column1: Replace 'Preview' page with 'Verify' page on the solvers App.
column30:
I would like to propose an enhancement to the current functionality of the solver's app, specifically concerning the preview page. The request is to replace the existing preview page with a new 'verify' page within the solver's app.

The primary purpose of this 'verify' page is to ensure the completeness of photo uploads to AWS. This feature will help in identifying any photos that have not been successfully uploaded to AWS, allowing solvers to promptly address any missing images by retaking them as needed.

The key objectives for implementing this 'Verify' page are as follows:

# Minimize the occurrence of lost photos by providing a comprehensive overview of uploaded and pending images.
# Enable solvers to easily identify and rectify any missing photos.

The 'Verify' page should be positioned as the final step in the photo-taking process, appearing after the solver has captured all the required images. On this page, solvers should have the ability to:

* Verify which photos have been successfully uploaded to AWS.
* Identify any photos that are yet to be uploaded, with these specific images being clearly highlighted for easy recognition.

column1: Add a check onClick(Pause) button that checks if the job submitted
column30:
When a solver experiences an issue during submission and they are not sure if the task submitted successfully, they Pause. But sometimes the form has submitted, then disappears from the schedule.  

We've been getting this scenario often (Paused job has disappeared from schedule). To improve on the submit functionality, we could add a check when the pause button is clicked that if request.status == complete, return an alert that the job has already been submitted and onClick(Ok) button, refreshes the schedule screen. That way the solver will know that the job submitted, and will improve the user experience.

|*vehicle size*|…|*valuation_charge*|
|small| |500|
|medium| |700|
|large| |1000|

So the amount the client gets asked to pay matches their vehicles size, and so the Mpesa code and comment input become redundant as the system will automatically pick the payment from the system and notify the solver.

Embed Zoho Analytics dashboard on current app
column30:
Future security suggestion:
Problem: We currently share the Zoho dashboard links as a public link both internally and to our channel partners, meaning that past, present and future employees ALL have access to the data as long as the link stays the same. The ideal situation would be to create an account for each dashboard user on Zoho, which would be expensive.
Proposed solution: Add a button on our main page called Analytics that redirects to solvit.com/dashboard etc, where a user authenticates with their work email, and based on that, are redirected to the respective company's dashboard. That way, only current authorised can access the data, and so that if an employee leaves, they no longer have access if their account is deactivated.
